Adding "winapp ui" for UI automation (#380)

## Description

Adds a new winapp ui command group that enables UI automation for
Windows apps using Microsoft UI Automation (UIA). This allows
developers and AI agents to programmatically discover, inspect, and
interact with UI elements across any Windows application.

## Subcommands

| Command | Description |
| ---------------- |
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
|
| ui list-windows | List visible windows for an app |
| ui status | Connect to an app and print session info |
| ui inspect | Dump the UI tree (or ancestors/subtree) |
| ui search | Find elements by text query |
| ui invoke | Activate an element via UIA patterns (Invoke, Toggle,
Select, ExpandCollapse) |
| ui click | Click via mouse input at screen coordinates (fallback for
non-invocable controls) |
| ui focus | Move keyboard focus to an element |
| ui set-value | Set text/value on editable controls |
| ui get-property | Read UIA property values from an element |
| ui get-focused | Report the currently focused element |
| ui screenshot | Save a PNG of a window or element |
| ui scroll | Scroll a container (up/down/left/right/top/bottom) |
| ui scroll-into-view | Scroll a target element into view |
| ui wait-for | Wait for element appearance, disappearance, or property
change |

## Key design decisions

- Semantic slug selectors — inspect and search output stable, shell-safe
element slugs (e.g., btn-close-d1a2) that can be passed
 directly to other commands
- Flexible app targeting — Target apps by process name, window title,
PID, or HWND (--window for stability)
- Invokable ancestor fallback — search and invoke can surface/use a
parent element that supports invoke when the matched child
does not
- Mouse input fallback — click uses real mouse injection (SetCursorPos +
SendInput) for controls that don't expose UIA invoke
patterns
- Testable architecture — UIA and session services are behind interfaces
with fakes for unit testing

## Example usage

 winapp ui inspect --app notepad
 winapp ui search "Save" --app myapp
 winapp ui invoke btn-save-d1a2 --app myapp
 winapp ui screenshot --app myapp --output screen.png
 winapp ui wait-for btn-done-f3c1 --app myapp --timeout 10

commit.message:
Add locks to buildtrees and packages. (#1979)

This is a followup on https://github.com/microsoft/vcpkg-tool/pull/1938
Resolves
https://github.com/microsoft/vcpkg/pull/51004#issuecomment-4195577101

@autoantwort points out that our MSBuild integration leaves the default
buildtrees location and only overrides the installed root:


https://github.com/microsoft/vcpkg/blob/81e3c888ef717a026eb57bb2f3cb0dbd30246271/scripts/buildsystems/msbuild/vcpkg.targets#L183-L190

I initially tried a solution which would get `buildtrees()` out of
`VcpkgPaths` entirely and pass around something that had both the data
and effectively encapsulated that things had been locked, but changes to
make that happen were starting to get huge, and I saw that I had already
accidentally created deadlocks by being inconsistent about the order of
taking the lock on the installed tree vs. buildtrees and packages, so I
gave up on it. (
https://github.com/BillyONeal/vcpkg-tool/commit/0a4b9ef757f68fd784bc14994bb7fb85ecd182eb
)

---------

commit.message:
Add 'winapp new' command for creating WinUI 3 projects and items (#411)

## Summary

Adds a new top-level \winapp new\ command that scaffolds WinUI 3
projects and items using the
\Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK.WinUI.CSharp.Templates\ package.

## What it does

- **Interactive mode**: Presents a template picker and name prompt when
run without arguments
- **Non-interactive mode**: \winapp new winui -n MyApp\ creates a
project with defaults
- **Context-aware**: Outside a \.csproj\ directory shows project
templates (winui, winui-navview, etc.); inside one shows item templates
(winui-page, winui-window, etc.)
- **Auto-installs/updates** the template package on each invocation
- **Dynamic discovery**: Reads template.json from the nupkg — no
hardcoded template names or parameters
- **Post-creation setup**: Configures RuntimeIdentifier for current arch
and adds the WinApp build tools NuGet package
- **Next steps**: Shows \cd <name>\ + \dotnet run\ after project
creation
- **Unique default names**: Avoids collisions with existing directories
(\winui-app\, \winui-app2\, etc.)

## Architecture

Introduces an \ITemplateProvider\ abstraction for future C++ template
support:

\\\
ITemplateProvider (per-language contract)
  └── DotNetTemplateProvider  (C# via dotnet new)
  └── [future] CppTemplateProvider

ITemplateService (composite, aggregates all providers)
  └── NewCommand talks to this
\\\

Adding a new language later is a single DI registration line.

## Usage examples

\\\ ash
# Interactive
winapp new

# Create a blank WinUI app
winapp new winui

# Create a NavigationView app with a name
winapp new winui-navview -n MyApp

# Add a page to an existing project (from inside the project dir)
winapp new winui-page -n SettingsPage

# Pass extra dotnet new arguments
winapp new winui -n MyApp -- --dotnet-version net9.0
\\\

## Files

### New (7 files)
- \Commands/NewCommand.cs\ — Command with interactive + non-interactive
flows
- \Services/ITemplateProvider.cs\ — Per-language provider interface
- \Services/DotNetTemplateProvider.cs\ — C# provider (reads nupkg, wraps
dotnet new)
- \Services/ITemplateService.cs\ — Composite service interface
- \Services/TemplateService.cs\ — Aggregates all providers
- \Models/TemplateInfo.cs\ — Template/parameter models + JSON source-gen
- \WinApp.Cli.Tests/NewCommandTests.cs\ — 15 tests

### Modified
- \WinAppRootCommand.cs\, \HostBuilderExtensions.cs\ — DI registration
- \README.md\, \docs/usage.md\, \docs/guides/dotnet.md\, setup skill —
Documentation
- Autogenerated: \cli-schema.json\, \
pm-usage.md\, \winapp-commands.ts\, \SKILL.md\

## Tests

15 new tests covering:
- CLI schema registration
- JSON deserialization (including trailing commas from real Microsoft
templates)
- Nupkg template discovery (project/item types, parameter extraction,
invalid templates)
- Composite service (provider aggregation, create routing, unknown
template errors)
- Unique default name generation (directory/file collision handling)
- Non-interactive error paths

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: correct YAML syntax in Maestro onFlowStart/onFlowComplete hooks

Moved onFlowStart and onFlowComplete lifecycle hooks from after the --- separator to before it (at the top level with other metadata like appId, name, tags). This fixes YAML parse errors that were causing flows to fail with 'expected <block end>, but found ?' errors.

The hooks must be at the same indentation level as appId/name/tags and must come before the --- separator that divides metadata from test commands.

Affected 22 flow files that had hooks in the wrong position.

commit.message:
fix: add Request-Id and Request-Context to CORS allowed headers, remove double-CORS in production (#227)

The Application Insights browser SDK (enableCorsCorrelation: true) sends
Request-Id and Request-Context headers on cross-origin requests. These
were missing from both the FastAPI CORSMiddleware and the Azure Container
App CORS policy, causing preflight failures.

Also removes CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS from the backend env vars in production
deployments to prevent double-CORS headers (Container App CORS handles
preflights at the proxy level; FastAPI CORSMiddleware adding a second set
of headers on actual requests risks duplicate Access-Control-Allow-Origin
values which browsers reject).

commit.message:
fix: pipeline login failure — trigger:none, remove YAML secret vars, fail-fast az login

Root cause: 'trigger: branches: [main]' fired an auto-triggered run when the
pipeline definition was created (main already had commits). That auto-run had
no runtime variables, so CROSSPLANE_SP_CLIENT_SECRET was empty → az login
used '--password ""' → 'Please specify both username and password' error.
The Validate stage passed despite this because terraform tests use mock_provider.

Fixes applied:
- trigger: none — bootstrap pipeline should only run when explicitly triggered
  (Backstage initial run, or ADO UI with secrets provided as queue-time vars)
- Remove CROSSPLANE_SP_CLIENT_SECRET and ARGOCD_ADO_PAT from YAML variables
  section — keeping them as empty strings could prevent queue-time override
  from taking effect on some ADO versions; now they exist only as Build
  Definition variables (allowOverride: true) created by the Backstage action
- Remove az login from Validate stage — all tests use mock_provider so no
  Azure credentials are needed there
- Add explicit empty-check + || exit 1 to az login in Provision stage so the
  pipeline fails fast with a clear error instead of continuing silently

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve ANR by moving blocking operations off main thread

The app was freezing after launch due to blocking database operations.
Root cause: SeedDatabaseCallback.onCreate() was creating a second Room
database instance synchronously, causing a recursive database creation
that blocked the main thread for 5+ seconds.

Solution:
- Removed the recursive Room.databaseBuilder().build() call
- Use SupportSQLiteDatabase instance passed to callback directly
- Execute seeding asynchronously on Dispatchers.IO
- Use raw SQL queries to avoid DAO initialization overhead

network: clear persisted bans for whitelisted peers on startup

attach_storage() was loading sled-persisted bans after the whitelist
had already been populated, causing whitelisted peers (masternodes,
config-trusted nodes) to re-acquire their bans every restart and lose
connectivity over time.

Fix: in all four load loops (permanent, temp, violations), skip any
entry whose IP is already in self.whitelist and remove it from sled so
it cannot accumulate. Log a 🔓 message for each cleared entry so
operators can see the cleanup on startup.

commit.message:
fix: set outputFileTracingRoot to repo root for Vercel adapter path resolution

With rootDirectory=frontend and postbuild copying .next/ to repo root,
Vercel's @vercel/next adapter resolves traced file paths relative to the
.next/ parent directory (/vercel/path0/). With outputFileTracingRoot=repoRoot,
traced paths are stored as frontend/node_modules/next/... (relative to repo
root), so the adapter correctly finds /vercel/path0/frontend/node_modules/next/.
